attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2. Then a detailed grep search was performed in SlotReels.ts file to understand the NearWin implementation

From the grep results, I can see:
- The codebase has NearWin functionality related to Scatter symbols
- There are constants like SCATTER_NEARWIN_COLLECT_COUNT, SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT
- There's tracking of scatter collection counts
- The code manages NearWin animations, effects, and sound
- There's logic for when NearWin is triggered (when scatter count >= 2 and < 5)

However, at this point, only investigation/grep has been performed. No actual files have been modified yet. The session is in the discovery phase - understanding where NearWin and vibration/shake functionality exists in the codebase.

aiTitle: NearWin 震動改為 Scatter 掉落時觸發

content.thinking:
The developer made the first code change! They edited SlotReels.ts to restrict the vibration in the StartFallSymbol method to only trigger when a Scatter symbol lands, not for all symbols during NearWin mode.

The change:
- Old: `// NearWin 模式下每顆落地都震動` and `if (this.m_isHasScatterNearWin)`
- New: `// NearWin 模式下只有 Scatter 落地才震動`, added a const to check if it's a scatter, and changed the condition to `if (this.m_isHasScatterNearWin && isScatter)`

This is a bugfix type observation since it changes existing behavior to match the requirement. This is the first of the three vibration points being fixed.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Restricted NearWin vibration to Scatter drops only - first trigger point</title>
  <subtitle>Modified StartFallSymbol to vibrate only when Scatter symbols land during NearWin mode</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>StartFallSymbol method in SlotReels.ts line 3196 previously vibrated for all symbols during NearWin mode</fact>
    <fact>Added isScatter check: symbolId === Game_Define.Symbol.Scatter || SilverScatter || GoldScatter</fact>
    <fact>Changed vibration condition from m_isHasScatterNearWin to m_isHasScatterNearWin && isScatter</fact>
    <fact>BigHintShake(0.3, Game_Define.NEAR_WIN_SHAKE_DISTANCE) now only fires for Scatter symbol drops</fact>
    <fact>Comment updated from "每顆落地都震動" (every symbol vibrates) to "只有 Scatter 落地才震動" (only Scatter vibrates)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>First of three vibration trigger points fixed in the NearWin system. The StartFallSymbol method handles symbol drop animations when new symbols fall from above the reels. Previously, during NearWin mode (when 2-4 Scatter symbols are visible and triggering anticipation effects), every symbol landing would trigger device vibration via BigHintShake. This created excessive vibration feedback. The fix adds a symbol type check to restrict vibration to only Scatter symbols (including SilverScatter and GoldScatter variants). The isScatter boolean is computed inline and combined with the existing NearWin state check, ensuring vibration only occurs when both conditions are met: NearWin is active AND the landing symbol is a Scatter. Two more vibration points remain to be fixed at lines 3608 and 3904.</narrative>

    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>

</observation>
```

aiTitle: NearWin 震動改為 Scatter 掉落時觸發

content.thinking:
The developer just modified the second vibration trigger point at line 3608 in the TileMatchingAndPatchUp method's CreateDropTween callback. This is part of the tile matching/cascade drop logic where existing symbols fall down to fill gaps after eliminations. The fix adds an isScatter check using the symId variable (which was already available in the closure) and restricts vibration to only Scatter symbols during NearWin mode.

This is the second of three vibration points being fixed. One more remains at line 3904 in the EnterPlate method.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Restricted NearWin vibration to Scatter drops only - second trigger point</title>
  <subtitle>Modified TileMatchingAndPatchUp to vibrate only when Scatter symbols land during tile matching drops</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>TileMatchingAndPatchUp method line 3608 CreateDropTween callback previously vibrated for all symbols during NearWin</fact>
    <fact>Added isScatter check using symId variable from outer scope to identify Scatter/SilverScatter/GoldScatter</fact>
    <fact>Changed condition from m_isHasScatterNearWin alone to m_isHasScatterNearWin && isScatter</fact>
    <fact>This trigger point handles tile matching cascade drops where existing symbols fall to fill eliminated positions</fact>
    <fact>Two of three vibration trigger points now fixed, third remains at line 3904 in EnterPlate method</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Second vibration trigger point modified in the NearWin system. The TileMatchingAndPatchUp method handles cascade elimination mechanics where symbols drop down to fill gaps after matches are cleared. Within its tile matching loop, symbols that need to move downward are animated via CreateDropTween with a callback that previously triggered vibration for all symbol types during NearWin mode. The fix leverages the symId variable (symbol ID of the dropping symbol) already available in the closure scope to compute an isScatter boolean, then combines it with the NearWin state check. This ensures vibration only fires when both conditions are met: NearWin is active AND the dropping symbol is specifically a Scatter variant. One more vibration point remains to be fixed in the EnterPlate method where new symbols drop from above during refill operations.</narrative>

    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```
